correctness.justification: All 17/17 acceptance tests pass. The implementation correctly handles atomic writes via tempfile + os.replace, supports both str and bytes content, creates parent directories, preserves permissions on existing files, and accepts both str and Path inputs.

edge_cases.justification: All edge cases are handled: empty string/bytes content, non-existent parent directories, existing file overwrite, permission preservation, TypeError for invalid content types, temp file cleanup on write failure, temp file cleanup on os.replace failure, and target file not created on failure.

code_quality.justification: Excellent code quality: full type hints including modern union syntax (str | Path, int | None), clear variable naming, clean structure with logical flow, proper use of pathlib, context managers for file handles, and idiomatic Python. Minor note: the `mode` parameter is accepted but not actually used (documented as 'for reference'), which is slightly misleading but documented.

documentation.justification: Comprehensive NumPy-style docstrings with clear description, Parameters section with types and descriptions, Raises section covering TypeError and OSError, and a working doctest example. Inline comments explain non-obvious decisions like 'rename succeeded; no cleanup needed' and capturing permissions before replacement.

test_coverage.justification: Thorough test suite covering: happy path (text, bytes, str path, Path path), parent directory creation, overwrite behavior, empty content (str and bytes), custom encoding, permission preservation, TypeError for int and list content, temp file cleanup on fdopen failure, temp file cleanup on os.replace failure, and target file not created on failure. Tests are well-named and use appropriate pytest fixtures.

robustness.justification: Validates content type with clear TypeError message, uses try/finally to guarantee temp file cleanup on any failure path, sets tmp_path to None after successful rename to avoid spurious cleanup, silently ignores OSError during cleanup (appropriate), creates parent directories automatically, and preserves existing file permissions. All failure modes are handled gracefully.
